This puncture-proof, non-pilling sack is designed to carry heavy payloads between 2.5 kg and 25 kg without tearing or stretching. item.g:title: Home Compostable CitrusCycle™ Orange Peel Waste PHA 3D Printing Filament
item.g:description:
Transform your packaging prototyping and dieline test fits with our Loopha PHA Sustainable 3D Printing Filament. Engineered from Polyhydroxyalkanoate (PHA) and upcycled organic waste, this high-performance material represents the next generation of bioplastics. It is available in 10 raw-material composites including Orange Peel, Coffee Grounds, Tea/Matcha, Grape skins, Peach, Bamboo, Oyster Shell, Charcoal, Rice, and Seaweed. Unlike standard PLA, which requires strict industrial composting and mandatory 4-hour pre-drying, Loopha PHA is natively home-compostable and marine-degradable. Crucially, it features a softening temperature above 60°C (PLA is 50-55°C), high toughness, low cooling shrinkage (allowing prints without a heated bed), and emits a subtle, pleasant organic scent during printing instead of chemical fumes.
Key Features & Specifications: • 10 Organic Composites: Choose from Orange, Coffee, Green Tea, Grape, Peach, Bamboo, Oyster Shell, Charcoal, Rice, or Seaweed, each showcasing unique natural textures and colors. • 6 Core Advantages: High thermal stability (>60°C), premium toughness, natural soil/marine degradation, safe & non-toxic (zero microplastics), low carbon footprint, and non-toxic organic aroma. • No Pre-Drying Bottleneck: Stable polymer structure allows printing straight out of the box without the 4-hour pre-drying required for PLA and PETG. • Zero Heated Bed Required: Low cooling shrinkage prevents warping, ensuring exceptional printability on standard FDM setups. • B2B Wholesale Tiers: Spooled in 1 kg rollstock format, optimized for eco-packaging prototyping and bio-plastics research.
Product Information: • Material composition: Compostable PLA • Available Sizing: 1 kg Spool (1.75 mm / 2.85 mm) • Production Turnaround: 7-10 Days • Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ): 10 units
